channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ethiopia Accelerates Economic Reforms Amid Debate Over Risks to Domestic Industries
item.description: Ethiopia is going through one of the biggest economic transitions in its recent history as Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed’s government pushes ahead with major liberalisation reforms. After many years of re...

item.title: Media analyst says CBS firing of veteran journalist Scott Pelley could trigger internal turmoil and legal dispute
item.description: A media controversy has emerged at CBS following the reported dismissal of veteran journalist Scott Pelley, who had worked at the network for more than four decades. According to the article, CBS exec...

item.title: Legal expert says Trump administration’s “weaponization fund” is Todd Blanche’s main political liability
item.description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche is facing increasing political scrutiny, but according to legal analyst Harry Litman, the controversy surrounding the Jeffrey Epstein files is not his most se...

item.title: Justice Sotomayor criticizes Supreme Court ruling in Alabama redistricting case over voting rights concerns
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Sonia Sotomayor issued a forceful dissenting opinion in a case concerning Alabama’s congressional district map, arguing that the Court’s majority failed to properly address serio...

item.title: President Samia Appoints Ummy Mwalimu as Presidential Advisor for Reproductive, Maternal and Child Health
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has appointed former Health Minister Ummy Mwalimu as Presidential Advisor on Reproductive, Maternal and Child Health. The appointment was announced on June 2, 2026, throu...

item.title: U.S. primary elections held across six states set stage for 2026 midterms
item.description: Primary elections were held across six U.S. states, drawing national attention as key contests began shaping the political landscape ahead of the November midterm elections. The races span California,...

item.title: Two convicted and sentenced to life imprisonment in Dar es Salaam heroin trafficking case; co-accused mother acquitted
item.description: The High Court of Tanzania sitting in Dar es Salaam has sentenced two residents to life imprisonment after finding them guilty of trafficking 15.19 kilogrammes of heroin, while acquitting a third accu...

item.title: Article Claims Scott Pelley Was Fired Following Internal Disputes at '60 Minutes'
item.description: The article reports that veteran CBS journalist Scott Pelley was allegedly fired from the news magazine program '60 Minutes' after a confrontation with newly appointed leadership. According to the rep...

item.title: Candace Owens Scheduled to Speak at Russian Economic Forum Alongside Sanctioned Russian Officials
item.description: Raw Story reports that conservative commentator and podcaster Candace Owens is scheduled to participate in a panel discussion at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum in Russia. According to...

item.title: CNN commentators criticize Bill Pulte's appointment as acting Director of National Intelligence
item.description: CNN commentators and political analysts expressed concern over President Donald Trump's appointment of Bill Pulte as acting Director of National Intelligence. Pulte, who currently serves as director o...

item.title: David Axelrod Says Iowa Senate Primary May Signal Democratic Prospects in the 2026 Midterms
item.description: Former Obama adviser David Axelrod highlighted Iowa's Democratic Senate primary as one of the most significant races to watch during Tuesday's elections, arguing that it could provide an early indicat...

item.title: Los Angeles mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt says he would return to his crystal business if he loses election
item.description: Spencer Pratt, the former reality television personality known for his role on 'The Hills,' is currently running as a Republican candidate for mayor of Los Angeles against incumbent Mayor Karen Bass. ...

item.title: Vintage Photographs Showcase the Everyday Lives of Young Women During the Edwardian Era
item.description: This article from Vintage Everyday presents a collection of historical photographs depicting young women during the Edwardian era, a period spanning the early 1900s and often remembered for its elegan...

item.title: Nigeria Raises Ebola Alert as DRC Outbreak Expands and Health Authorities Strengthen Preparedness
item.description: This opinion article highlights the growing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and the implications for Nigeria and other neighbouring countries. The author warns against com...

item.title: Largest Genetic Analysis of Alzheimer's Disease Reveals 48 Previously Unreported Risk Regions
item.description: A comprehensive genetic study, the largest of its kind analyzing common variants in Alzheimer's disease, examined data from over 2.8 million individuals across multiple ancestry groups. Researchers id...

item.title: Texas Secretary of State Jane Nelson Announces Resignation Amid Debate Over Closed Primaries
item.description: Texas Secretary of State Jane Nelson announced that she will resign from her position effective July 17, ending a tenure of nearly four years overseeing the state's election administration. In her res...

item.title: MK Party suspends Mmabatho Mokoena-Zondi and removes her as chief whip following fraud allegations
item.description: The MK Party has removed its parliamentary chief whip, Mmabatho Mokoena-Zondi, and suspended her party membership with immediate effect after she was charged with fraud involving more than R230,000. A...

item.title: Police Respond to Hostage Situation at Downtown Bakersfield Chase Bank After Suspect Claims to Have Explosive Device
item.description: Authorities in Bakersfield, California, responded to a major hostage situation on June 2, 2026, after a man entered a Chase Bank in the city’s downtown area and reportedly claimed to have a bomb strap...

item.title: Rare Early 20th-Century Postcards Showcase the Life and Career of Austrian Silent Film Star Mia May
item.description: The article presents a collection of 26 vintage postcards featuring Austrian actress Mia May, one of the prominent stars of early German silent cinema. Born Hermine Pfleger in Vienna in 1884, she demo...

item.title: Former political aide files lawsuit over alleged dating app catfishing and covert recordings
item.description: A former senior political aide to New Jersey Governor Phil Murphy has filed a lawsuit alleging he was the victim of an elaborate deception involving a dating app relationship that turned into a covert...
